explanation: The property states that from time instant 2 onwards (i.e., after the DM is started and 2 time units have passed), it must always be the case that 'dm_without_master' (a DM remaining without a DM master associated) does not hold, i.e., it is forbidden for a DM to be without a DM master associated after 2 time units. This captures the requirement that as soon as the DM is started, it may be briefly without a master, but after 2 time units it must always have a master associated.

The property requires that, starting from 2 time units after a DM starts (which is modeled as when the system starts, as there is no explicit 'DM started' proposition), it is always the case that the DM does not remain without a DM master associated. This captures the requirement that a DM cannot be in the 'without master' state after 2 time units from its start, and formalizes it as: for all times starting at t=2, dm_without_master must be false.

The requirement specifies that it is not allowed for any DM to remain without an associated DM master for more than 2 time units after the DM is started. In LTL, this is best modeled as: 'After 2 time units, it is always the case that a DM is not without a master.' Essentially, beginning at t=2 and onward, 'dm_without_master' must never hold (i.e., must always be false). This matches the structure of template 2 ('G[N_DURATION0,] !bool_exp3'), where '!dm_without_master' is enforced from time 2 on. Hence, the formula blocks cases where a DM might remain without a master from time 2 after its start and onward, directly expressing the intended safety requirement.

The requirement states that it is not allowed for a DM to remain without a DM master associated for more than 2 time units after it has started (which we assume means from system start). This translates to 'dm_without_master' being forbidden at any time after the first 2 time units. The most direct encoding uses the template that forbids a proposition globally after some duration from the beginning. Here, for any time t ≥ 2, dm_without_master must not hold. Therefore, we use template 2: G[N_DURATION0,] !bool_exp3, with bool_exp3 set to dm_without_master and N_DURATION0 set to 2.

This LTL property expresses that it is always the case that within 2 time units from any moment onwards, it is not true that a DM remains without a DM master associated. This directly encodes the requirement that a DM cannot remain without a DM master associated after 2 time units it is started; after 2 time units, this condition must not persist.

The property expresses that after a DM is started (i.e., from time 0), it is not allowed for the DM to be without a DM master associated after more than 2 time units. This means that, globally, from time 2 onwards, the DM cannot be without a master. This is captured by applying the globally operator (G) from time 2 to infinity to the negation of the 'dm_without_master' atomic proposition, ensuring that after the initial 2 time units it is never true that a DM is without a master.

The requirement states that a DM cannot remain without a DM master associated after 2 time units it is started. This means, at any time, it is not allowed for a DM to be without a master for more than 2 time units. In LTL, this is expressed as 'it is always the case that dm_without_master does not hold for more than 2 time units'. Thus, we require that 'dm_without_master' is never true for more than 2 time units in any trace. This is best captured by the globally bounded response template G[0,2] !dm_without_master, which states that within every sliding window of length 2 (from now, and for the next 2 time units), 'dm_without_master' must never be true. This enforces that a DM cannot persist in the 'without master' state for over 2 consecutive time units.

The requirement specifies that a DM (Device Manager) cannot stay in a state where it has no DM master associated for more than 2 time units after it is started. Assuming 'started' means the DM comes into existence or is active, we must express as an LTL property: 'It is not allowed for the system to be in a state where dm_without_master holds for more than 2 time units consecutively at any time.' This fits the template 'Globally, from time t=0, dm_without_master is not enabled for more than N_DURATION1 time units', which corresponds to 'G[0,N_DURATION1] !dm_without_master' (template 1). Here, N_DURATION1 is set to 2 to match the required bound.

The property states that it is globally forbidden for a DM to remain without a DM master associated for more than 2 time units after it is started. The natural language explicitly states that after starting, a DM cannot be without a DM master for 2 time units or longer, so the formula enforces that the 'dm_without_master' proposition (which captures 'a DM remains without a DM master associated') must not hold in the time interval starting from time 2 onwards. Thus, the formula G[2,] !dm_without_master is used, which ensures that starting from time 2, it is always the case that the DM is not in a state without a master.

The requirement states that a DM cannot stay in the state where it has no DM master associated more than 2 time units after it is started. This means that the condition 'dm_without_master' cannot last for more than 2 time units after system (or DM instance) startup. Therefore, we must ensure that 'dm_without_master' cannot persist globally after 2 time units. Template 2 (G[N_DURATION0,] !bool_exp3) captures this: it enforces that, from time unit 2 onwards, it is always NOT the case that the DM remains without a DM master associated ('dm_without_master' is false). We set N_DURATION0=2 accordingly.
